title: Why lifetimes exist
instruction:
This code would cause a dangling reference — a reference to freed data.

Rust refuses to compile it. Run it to see the error.
hint: The reference r outlives the data it points to. The data is dropped inside the inner scope.

codeChecks.message: x is dropped before r is used. Move x outside the inner scope to fix this.

successMessage: The borrow checker rejected this. r would have outlived x. This kind of bug causes crashes in C/C++. Rust catches it at compile time.

title: Simple lifetime annotation
instruction:
When a function returns a reference derived from its inputs, Rust needs to know which input it comes from.

Run this.
successMessage: 'a says 'the returned reference lives at least as long as the shorter of x and y'. The annotation constrains the lifetime.

title: Lifetime elision rules
instruction:
Rust has three lifetime elision rules to avoid annotation boilerplate.

If the compiler can apply them, no annotations needed.

Run this.
successMessage: No lifetime annotations needed here. The elision rules infer: the output reference has the same lifetime as the single input reference.

title: Structs with lifetime annotations
instruction:
If a struct holds a reference, it needs a lifetime parameter.

Run this.
successMessage: 'a annotates the struct: 'an instance of Important cannot outlive the data in text'. The struct borrows the string — it doesn't own it.

title: Multiple lifetime parameters
instruction:
Sometimes inputs have different lifetimes.

Run this.
successMessage: 'a and 'b are separate lifetimes. result has lifetime 'a (tied to ann), not 'b (tied to name). So result is valid after name is dropped.

title: Static lifetime
instruction:
`'static` means the reference lives for the entire program duration.

String literals are `'static` — they are baked into the binary.

Run this.
expectedOutput: hello world | I live forever in the binary
successMessage: String literals have 'static lifetime because they're embedded in the binary. They are valid for the entire program run.

title: Lifetime bounds on generics
instruction:
Generic types can have lifetime constraints too.

Run this.
successMessage: T: Display + 'a means T must implement Display AND must live at least as long as 'a. Lifetime bounds compose with trait bounds.

title: Lifetime summary
instruction:
Lifetimes ensure references never outlive the data they point to.

Run this summary program.
expectedOutput: longer: I am the longer string
successMessage: Lifetime annotations are not changing anything about how long data lives — they are describing relationships. The borrow checker uses them to verify safety.
